hypotheses.name: Two-segment org: Front-of-house / Back-of-house
hypotheses.agreement_summary: Layer 2 weakly supports two clusters; Layer 3 modularity peaks near a 2-community partition.
hypotheses.divergence_summary: Layer 1 folder shape suggests three branches; back-of-house may further split into ops vs finance.
hypotheses.narrative_argument_for: Two clusters maximize modularity at the chosen Leiden seed.
hypotheses.narrative_argument_against: ARI variance across seeds indicates the boundary is fragile.

hypotheses.name: Three-segment org: Customer / Finance / Operations
hypotheses.agreement_summary: Folder hierarchy clearly shows three top-level branches.
hypotheses.divergence_summary: Embedding clustering does not separate customer from operations cleanly.
hypotheses.narrative_argument_for: Filesystem provides strong prior for three groups.
hypotheses.narrative_argument_against: Layer 2/3 evidence is too weak to discriminate this from the 2-segment view.

hypotheses.name: Null hypothesis: undifferentiated whole
hypotheses.narrative_argument_for: Given seed-to-seed instability the data may not support any partition.
hypotheses.narrative_argument_against: Folder shape is informative even if embeddings are ambiguous.
